english: lodged, to be received into another's house Tar.; *ne-tsaṅ ǰuṅ* (ne tshang 'byung) C. you will be lodged here, you may stay here (over night), W. *dáṅ-sa* (dang sa). — γnas-tsúl (gnas tshul) 1. the state in which one is, good or bad, condition of life, séms-kyi (sems kyi) the state of one's soul or heart. 2. an account, of one's state of mind. 3. story, tale, narration; event, col. 4. in philosophy: the reality of being (opp. to non-existence) Was. (297). — γnas-γži (gnas gzhi) 1. = γnas (gnas) 3, Tar. frq. 2. the locative, that case which relates to being in or at a place Gram. — γnás-lugs (gnas lugs) 1. position, disposition, arrangement, lús-kyi (lus kyi) arrangement of the parts of the body, the science of anatomy Med. 2. in mystical works: γnás-lugs rtógs-pa (gnas lugs rtogs pa) the knowledge of the essence of things, the knowledge of all things, or in a Buddhist sense, of the non-existence of all things, Tar. and elsewh. — γnas-bšád (gnas bshad) 1. topography and geography col. 2. narration of legendary tales connected with some holy place. — γnás-sa (gnas sa) (v. γnás-pa (gnas pa)) the permanent residence of a person, or the constant place of a thing, opp. to *bór-sa* (bor sa) W. temporary place or residence; place, room, in general, *né-sa yáṅ-pa dug* (ne sa yang pa dug) W. there is much room here. — γnas-bsrúṅ (gnas bsrung) 1. W. ('locum tenens') earnest, earnest-money, pledge, security; it might also be used for ticket, ticket of admission etc. 2. Sch.: guardian, or warden of a monastery.

1. v. γnaṅ-ba (gnang ba). — 2. consciousness of guilt, γnoṅ laṅ (gnong lang) (his) conscience smites (him) Mil.; ogyod-ciṅ γnoṅ bkúr-bai sems ('gyod cing gnong bkur ba'i sems) repentance and a sense of guilt Dzl.

1. to be conscious of one's guilt, to feel remorse, to be stung in one's conscience, γnóṅ-žiṅ ogyód-pai sgó-nas (gnong zhing 'gyod pa'i sgo nas) from a consciousness of guilt Pth., γnoṅ-ogyód drág-pos (gnong 'gyod drag pos) id. Pth.; *nóṅ-ṅo láṅ-na ṭím-čọ́ de* (nong ngo lang na khrims gcod bde) C. where there is repentance, it is easy to pass judgment. — 2. to be seized with anguish, as the effect of poisoning. —

1. vb. (cf. snád-pa (snad pa)) to hurt, harm, injure, damage, rkáṅ-pa-la γnód-par ogyúr-gyi dógs-pas (rkang pa la gnod par 'gyur gyi dogs pas) in order not to hurt one's foot Dzl.; γnód-par ogyúr-bai dgra (gnod par 'gyur ba'i dgra) a dangerous enemy Dzl.; *ṅá-la nod yin* (nga la nod yin) W. (he or it) will hurt me. — More frq.: 2. sbst. damage, harm, injury, byéd-pa (byed pa), skyél-ba (skyel ba), Glr., Mil., *kyál-če* (skyel ce) W. to do harm, to inflict injury, to hurt, with la (la); γnód-pa med-par (gnod pa med par), ma ogyur-nas (ma 'gyur nas) without any harm, without injury Sch.; γnod-byed-nyés-pa (gnod byed nyes pa) v. nyés-pa (nyes pa) I. — klui γnód-pa (klu'i gnod pa) damage done by Nagas. — γnod-sbyín (gnod sbyin), यक्ष, a class of demons.
Sch.: falsehood, calumny; W. *nad-nád čó-k'aṅ* (nad nad bco mkhan) one doing damage maliciously.
resp. for gyón-pa (gyon pa), to put on, ná-bza (na bza') Lex. the garment; v. also nabs (nabs).
Cs. mean, worthless; Lex. and Sch.: nourishment, food, mnab-rtsál-gyi bu(-tsa) (mnab rtsal gyi bu (-tsha)) Cs.: the child of an indigent person, Sch.: foster-child; the word is not much known.

to smell of, cca., drí-ma glá-bai ril-ma mnam (dri ma gla ba'i ril ma mnam) as to its smell, it smells of the dung of a musk-deer; to smell agreeably, to exhale fragrance, e.g. the scent of lotus Glr.; more frq. to smell badly, to spread an offensive smell, to stink, rṅul maṅ dri mnam (rngul mang dri mnam) profuse and badly smelling perspiration Lt.; lus btsóg-pa mnám-pa (lus btsog pa mnam pa) (or -po (-po)) odi ('di) Dzl. this foul stinking body. Note: The transitive signification (to smell = to perceive by the nose) belongs only to the form snám-pa (snam pa), and Dzl. v, 14 should be translated: the medicine stank.

oath, mna obór-ba, odór-ba, byéd-pa, skyél-ba (mna' 'bor ba, 'dor ba, byed pa, skyel ba) B., *kyál-če* (skyel ce) W., to take an oath, to swear; lha dpáṅ-du btsúgs-nas mna byéd-pa (lha dpang du btsugs nas mna' byed pa) to swear by the lha Glr.; odi-skad čes mna bór-ro ('di skad ces mna' bor ro) Dzl.; bar daṅ mná- (bar dang mna'-)
